summary: Smoking Help>Slip>Frank sequence and soaring Terrapin Station
blurb: The first set rides a tight, energetic groove while the second set launches into a blistering Help On The Way > Slipknot > Franklin's Tower and a triumphant Terrapin Station. Brent’s laser‑organ shines and the drums/space segment propels an emotional Morning Dew.
review: April 1, 1984 at Marin County Veterans Auditorium captured one of the spring ’84 high‑water marks for the Dead. The band opened with crisp renditions of Jack Straw, Friend Of The Devil and Cassidy that set a bright tone, then built momentum into a first‑set climax on My Brother Esau. Nothing compared to the second‑set fireworks: a smoking Help On The Way → Slipknot → Franklin's Tower that had the crowd howling, followed by a towering Terrapin Station where Jerry’s smoky lead and Brent’s laser‑organ intertwined like never before. The Drums/Space segues were tight and powerful, leading into an emotionally charged Morning Dew that left listeners “melting.” Even the encore’s Touch of Grey, while a tad sloppy after the marathon night, couldn’t dim the overall brilliance. Across multiple soundboard and audience sources the performance is consistently praised for its energy, creativity, and tight interplay.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Six of eight recordings are strongly positive, repeatedly highlighting the Help On The Way > Slipknot > Franklin's Tower sequence, Terrapin Station, and Morning Dew. No consistent negative comments about playing quality appear; only two recordings note technical flaws unrelated to performance.

best_recording.reason: A‑plus soundboard capture with a well‑balanced mix, crisp laser‑organ, no hiss, and flawless coverage of the entire show.

key_highlights: Smoking Help On The Way > Slipknot > Franklin's Tower sequence.
Terrapin Station triumph with soaring guitar and organ interplay.
Powerful Drums/Space segues leading into an emotional Morning Dew.
Tight first‑set energy highlighted by Jack Straw, Friend Of The Devil, and Cassidy.

band_performance.Jerry: Creative, energetic playing with a smoky tone; soaring leads on Terrapin Station and an over‑the‑top solo on Morning Dew keep the band locked in.
band_performance.Phil: Roller‑blading bass lines that lock tightly with the rhythm section, especially strong during Esau and The Dew jams.
band_performance.Bob: Aggressive, swirling solos add fire to Terrapin Station; his slide chords on Samson & Delilah are cruelly effective and drive the set’s excitement.
band_performance.Brent: Laser‑organ front‑and‑center, inventive and often turned up high (especially on Esau); sweet melodic lines throughout the Help > Slip > Franklin sequence and jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Strong percussion that fuels the Samson & Delilah drum segment and powers the expansive Drums/Space section.
band_performance.Billy: Solid, driving beats that lock with Mickey; together they provide a powerful backbone for the second‑set improvisations.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
